title: Avocado Toast
description: A healthy and satisfying toast topped with creamy avocado, fresh herbs, and a touch of lemon — perfect for your morning boost!
ingredients:
2 slices of whole grain bread
1 ripe avocado
Salt & pepper to taste
Chili flakes (optional)
Fresh lemon juice
instructions:
1. Toast the bread slices until golden.
2. Mash the avocado in a bowl and mix with salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
3. Spread the mixture onto the toasted bread.
4. Top with chili flakes or herbs if desired. Serve immediately.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Veggie Omelette
ingredients:
2 eggs
2 tbsp milk (optional, makes it fluffy)
Salt – to taste
Black pepper – to taste
1–2 tsp oil or butter
2 tbsp onion
2 tbsp tomato (de-seeded)
2 tbsp bell peppers (any color)
1 tbsp spinach or coriander leaves
1 tbsp grated carrot
1 green chili (optional, finely chopped)
instructions:
1. Crack the eggs into a bowl. Add salt, pepper, and milk. Whisk well until frothy.
2. Add all the chopped veggies into the egg mixture. Mix everything together.
3. Heat oil or butter in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
4. Pour the egg-vegetable mixture into the pan. Spread it evenly.
5. Cover the pan with a lid and cook for 3–4 minutes until the bottom is set.
6. Flip carefully and cook the other side for 1–2 minutes.
7. Fold and serve hot with toast, ketchup, or green chutney.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Masala Dosa
ingredients:
2 cups rice
½ cup urad dal (split black gram)
2 tbsp chana dal (optional, for crispiness)
1 tbsp fenugreek seeds
Salt – to taste
Water – as needed
3–4 boiled potatoes (mashed roughly)
1 onion (sliced)
1 green chili (slit or chopped)
½ tsp mustard seeds
½ tsp cumin seeds
A few curry leaves
¼ tsp turmeric powder
instructions:
Dosa Batter Preparation:
1. Wash and soak rice, urad dal, chana dal, and fenugreek seeds in water for 6 hours.
2. Grind to a smooth batter adding water as needed.
3. Let it ferment overnight or for 8–12 hours in a warm place.
4. Add salt to the batter just before making dosas.
Potato Masala Preparation:
1. Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
2. Add cumin, chana dal, urad dal, curry leaves, and green chili..
3. Add sliced onions and sauté till translucent.
4. Add turmeric and salt. Mix in mashed potatoes.
5. Add little water if it's too dry. Cook for 2–3 minutes.
6. Garnish with coriander leaves. Set aside.
Masala Dosa Making:
1. Heat a non-stick or cast iron tawa and grease it lightly.
2. Pour a ladle of batter and spread it in a circular motion to make a thin dosa.
3. Drizzle oil or ghee around the edges. Cook until golden and crispy.
4. Place 2–3 tbsp of potato masala in the center. Fold and serve.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Quinoa Salad
ingredients:
1 cup cooked quinoa (about ½ cup uncooked)
½ cup chopped cucumber
½ cup chopped tomatoes (deseeded)
¼ cup grated carrot
¼ cup boiled corn or chickpeas (optional)
2 tbsp chopped fresh coriander or parsley
1 tbsp crumbled feta cheese or paneer (optional)
instructions:
1. Rinse ½ cup quinoa under running water. Cook it in 1 cup water until soft (about 10–12 minutes). Let it cool completely.
2. While quinoa cools, chop all vegetables and herbs.
3. In a large bowl, combine the cooked quinoa, chopped veggies, herbs, and cheese (if using).
4. Whisk together all dressing ingredients. Pour over the salad and toss everything well.
5. Let it rest for 5–10 minutes for the flavors to absorb. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Lentil Soup
ingredients:
1 cup lentils (red, green, or brown)
1 tbsp olive oil or ghee
1 medium onion (chopped)
2–3 garlic cloves (minced)
1 carrot (chopped)
1 tomato (chopped) or ½ cup canned tomatoes
½ tsp turmeric powder
1 tsp cumin powder
½ tsp black pepper
Salt – to taste
1 tbsp lemon juice (optional)
Fresh coriander or parsley for garnish
instructions:
1. Rinse lentils thoroughly under running water. Set aside.
2. In a deep soup pot, heat oil or ghee over medium heat.
Add onions and garlic. Sauté for 2–3 minutes until soft.
3. Add chopped carrot and tomatoes. Cook for another 2–3 minutes.
4. Add turmeric, cumin, salt, and pepper. Stir well.
5. Add rinsed lentils and pour in vegetable broth or water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 25–30 minutes or until lentils are soft.
6. Use a hand blender to blend partially (optional) for a creamy texture, or leave it chunky.
7. Add lemon juice and adjust seasoning if needed.
8. Garnish with fresh coriander or parsley. Serve hot with bread or rice.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Black Forest Cake
ingredients:
1½ cups all-purpose flour
1 cup sugar
⅓ cup unsweetened cocoa powder
1 tsp baking powder
½ tsp baking soda
½ tsp salt
½ cup oil
1 cup milk
1 tbsp vinegar or lemon juice
1 tsp vanilla extract
½ cup hot water or coffee (optional, enhances flavor)
1 tsp vanilla extract
1½ cups cherries (fresh or canned)
3–4 tbsp cherry syrup or juice (from canned cherries or sugar syrup)
Chocolate shavings or curls
instructions:
Baking the Chocolate Cake:
1. Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Grease and line two 8-inch cake pans.
2. In a large bowl, mix flour, sugar, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
3. Add oil, milk, vinegar, and vanilla. Mix until combined.
4. Stir in hot water or coffee (batter will be thin).
5. Pour into pans and bake for 30–35 minutes. Cool completely.
Prepare the Whipped Cream:
1. Beat chilled whipping cream with sugar and vanilla until stiff peaks form. Chill until ready to use.
Prepare the Cherry Filling:
1. If using fresh cherries, pit and chop them. If using canned, drain and save the syrup.
2. Optional: Heat cherries with a little sugar and cornstarch for a thicker filling.
Assemble the Cake:
1. Slice cake layers horizontally (if only one was baked).
2. Place the first layer on a cake board. Brush with cherry syrup.
3. Spread whipped cream and scatter cherries.
4. Add second layer. Repeat the process.
5. Cover the entire cake with whipped cream.
Decorate:
1. Press chocolate shavings on the sides.
2. Pipe cream swirls on top and place cherries.
3.Chill for 1–2 hours before serving for best texture.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Chocolate Lava Cake
ingredients:
½ cup (115g) unsalted butter
100g dark chocolate (chopped or chocolate chips)
2 eggs
¼ cup (50g) sugar
2 tbsp all-purpose flour
1 tsp vanilla extract (optional)
A pinch of salt
Butter and cocoa powder for greasing ramekins
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F).

Grease 2–4 ramekins (or cupcake molds) with butter and dust with cocoa powder. Shake out the excess.
2. In a bowl, melt butter and chocolate together using a double boiler or microwave. Stir until smooth. Let it cool slightly.
3. In another bowl, beat eggs, egg yolks, and sugar until light and fluffy (about 2–3 minutes).

Stir in the melted chocolate mixture.

Add flour, salt, and vanilla. Mix until just combined.
4. Pour batter into the greased ramekins, filling them ¾ full.

Bake for 10–12 minutes. The edges should be firm, but the center should be soft.
5. Let them cool for 1 minute. Run a knife around the edges and invert onto a plate.

Serve warm with vanilla ice cream, whipped cream, or berries.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Donuts
ingredients:
2 ¼ tsp active dry yeast (1 packet)
1 cup sugar
⅓ cup unsweetened cocoa powder
1 tsp baking powder
½ tsp baking soda
½ tsp salt
½ cup oil
1 cup milk
instructions:
1. In a bowl, mix warm milk, sugar, and yeast. Let it sit for 5–10 minutes until frothy.
2. In a large bowl, mix flour and salt. Add in the yeast mixture, egg, and butter.

Knead the dough for 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic (you can use a stand mixer or hands).

Cover the bowl with a damp cloth and let it rise for 1–1.5 hours until doubled in size.
3. Roll the dough to about ½-inch thickness.

Use a donut cutter (or a glass and bottle cap) to cut out donuts and holes.

Place on parchment paper and cover loosely. Let them rest for 30–40 minutes.
4. Heat oil to 180°C (350°F).

Fry 2–3 donuts at a time until golden brown (about 1 minute per side).

Drain on paper towels.
5. While warm, dip in sugar, glaze, or coat with cinnamon sugar.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Kheer
ingredients:
1/4 cup basmati rice (or any short-grain rice)
1 cup sugar
4–5 cardamom pods (crushed) or 1/2 tsp cardamom powder
8–10 cashews (chopped)
8–10 almonds (sliced or chopped)
1 tsp ghee (optional, for roasting nuts)
A few saffron strands (optional)
instructions:
1. Wash the rice thoroughly and soak it in water for 15–20 minutes. Drain before cooking.
2. In a heavy-bottomed pan, bring milk to a boil.

Lower the heat and simmer.
3. Add soaked rice to the milk and stir well.

Cook on low heat, stirring frequently to avoid sticking or burning.
4. Simmer for 30–40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until rice is soft and milk is thickened.
5. Add sugar, cardamom, and saffron (if using).

Stir and simmer for 5 more minutes.
6. In a small pan, heat ghee and lightly roast cashews, almonds, and raisins.

Add them to the kheer and mix well.
7. Serve warm or chilled, as preferred. Garnish with a few more nuts or saffron on top.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Chilli Potato
ingredients:
3 potatoes (cut into thin sticks)
3 tbsp cornflour
1 green chili (chopped)
¼ tsp turmeric
Fresh coriander – for garnish
½ tsp salt
½ cup oil
2 tbsp soy sauce
1 tbsp tomato ketchup
instructions:
1. Boil potato sticks for 2–3 mins. Drain, coat with cornflour, and deep-fry until crispy.
2. In a pan, sauté garlic and green chili. Add sauces, salt, pepper, and a splash of water.
3. Add fried potatoes, mix well to coat. Garnish with spring onions and serve hot!
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Garlic Cheese Bread
ingredients:
4 slices of bread or 1 small baguette (halved)
2 tbsp butter (softened)
1–2 tsp garlic (minced or grated)
1 tsp dried oregano or mixed herbs
Chopped coriander or parsley (optional)
instructions:
1. Mix softened butter with garlic and herbs.
2. Spread garlic butter over bread slices. Sprinkle cheese generously on top.
3. Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 8–10 mins until cheese melts and edges are crispy.
4. Garnish with parsley/coriander and enjoy with ketchup or dip!
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Corn Fritters
ingredients:
1 cup sweet corn (boiled or frozen)
¼ cup all-purpose flour (maida)
2 tbsp rice flour (for crispiness)
1 green chili (finely chopped)
1 small onion (chopped)
2 tbsp coriander leaves (chopped)
½ tsp cumin seeds
Salt – to taste
Water – as needed
Oil – for frying
instructions:
1. Coarsely crush half of the corn. Mix with flour, rice flour, chili, onion, coriander, cumin, and salt.
Add a little water to form a thick batter.
2. Heat oil in a pan. Drop spoonfuls of batter and flatten slightly.
Fry on medium heat until golden and crisp on both sides.
3. Enjoy with mint chutney, ketchup, or curd dip!
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Chicken Nuggets
ingredients:
250g boneless chicken (cut into small pieces or minced)
1 tsp garlic paste
½ tsp black pepper
Salt – to taste
¼ cup all-purpose flour
1 egg (beaten)
½ cup breadcrumbs (or crushed cornflakes for extra crunch)
Oil – for deep or shallow frying
instructions:
1. Mix chicken with garlic paste, salt, and pepper. Let sit for 10 minutes.
2. Dredge chicken pieces in flour.

Dip into the beaten egg.

Roll in breadcrumbs to coat evenly.
3. Heat oil in a pan. Fry nuggets on medium heat until golden brown and cooked inside (about 3–4 minutes per side).
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Masala Chai
ingredients:
1½ cups water
1 cup milk (adjust as preferred)
2 tsp black tea leaves (or 2 tea bags)
2–3 tsp sugar (or to taste)
Masala
A pinch of cardamom (optional, for flavor)
instructions:
1. In a saucepan, add water and the whole spices. Let it boil for 3–4 minutes so the flavors infuse.
2. Add tea leaves and milk. Bring it to a gentle boil.
3. Simmer for 2–3 minutes until the chai turns a rich color and aroma develops.
4. Add sugar and stir well. Simmer for another 30 seconds.
5. Strain into cups and enjoy your hot masala chai!
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Lemon-Iced Tea
ingredients:
2 cups water
2 black tea bags (or 2 tsp loose tea)
2 tsp sugar or honey (adjust to taste)
Juice of 1 lemon
Ice cubes – as needed
Lemon slices & mint leaves – for garnish (optional)
instructions:
1. Bring 2 cups of water to a boil. Turn off the heat.
2. Add tea bags and let them steep for 3–5 minutes, depending on how strong you want it. Then remove tea bags
3. While the tea is still warm, stir in sugar or honey until dissolved.
4. Let the tea cool slightly, then add fresh lemon juice.
5. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. Serve over ice cubes. Garnish with lemon slices or mint leaves if desired.
author: TastyTrove Chef

title: Mango Smoothie
ingredients:
1 large ripe mango (peeled & chopped)
½ cup yogurt or curd
½ cup chilled milk (or water for lighter version)
1–2 tsp honey or sugar (optional, adjust to taste)
A few ice cubes (optional)
A pinch of cardamom (optional, for flavor)
instructions:
1. Add mango, yogurt, milk, sweetener, and ice to a blender.
2. Blend until creamy and lump-free.
3. Pour into glasses. Garnish with mango cubes or mint (optional). Enjoy cold!
author: TastyTrove Chef
